summary: McKenzie analyzes Tether's November 2022 attestation and argues that, even taking their own numbers at face value, Tether was again insolvent. With only $250 million in equity against $68 billion in liabilities and 12.86% of reserves in risk-on assets, a mere 2.86% impairment would wipe out their cushion — an impossibility to avoid given Bitcoin's 10%+ decline. He notes their accountants' careful disclaimers about extraordinary market conditions, their historical largest counterparty Alameda Research being vaporized, and the mystery of where $120 million/month in Treasury interest income disappears to. The piece frames Tether as the largest fraud in history, drawing explicit parallels to Madoff's unraveling.
key_points: Tether's own attestation shows only 0.36% equity-to-assets ratio with 270:1 debt-to-equity, meaning even trivial impairment of risk-on holdings causes insolvency
Their accountants explicitly disclaim the valuations under 'extraordinary market conditions' — exactly the conditions occurring during the FTX/Alameda collapse
Tether generates ~$120 million/month in Treasury interest yet maintains only $250 million in equity, raising serious questions about where the money goes
The crypto ecosystem may have appetite but not capacity to bail out Tether, since systemic crises drain the very dry powder needed for rescue
Tether's press releases quietly stopped claiming to be 'fully backed,' shifting focus from solvency to liquidity
money_quotes: Their equity is already vapor. They are leveraged 35:1 against their risk-on assets alone.
I challenge anyone to find a portfolio construction for digital token investments that possibly survived this environment with only a 2.86% impairment.
Tether is a gigantic piggy bank, run by confessed liars.
Their debt to equity ratio of about 270:1 would place them comfortably in the leagues of the firms that were at ground zero of the global financial crisis in 2008.
Where is it, though. How do they only have $250 million in equity if just their Treasuries are generating about $120 million in interest a month?
Tether's accountants carefully avoid saying that Tether's reserves are adequate, presumably because they like money but do not like the prospect of jail.

Even if we believe Tether's reserves report for the sake of argument, and we grant them very favorable assumptions as to their asset mixes and sagacity as traders, we still arrive at their reserves having again become insufficient. Their own numbers indict them.
Madoff lasted more than 17 years. Tether won't.
key_insight: Tether's own published attestation, even if taken entirely at face value, mathematically demonstrates insolvency given any non-trivial market decline — their 0.36% equity cushion cannot survive the 12.86% of reserves held in risk-on assets.
